evals.expected_output: A friendly plain-English narrative explaining the NVDA model output. Should answer five questions: (1) what the number says — current price vs base IV with % gap, (2) how it was built — FCF starting point with SBC strip explained, growth rate source, WACC, two-stage structure, (3) what drives it — WACC sensitivity in dollar terms with interest-rate framing, plus implied year-5 FCF figure, (4) failure modes (playbook-loaded for NVDA), (5) confidence with qualitative business context (market lifecycle, what kind of bet the base case is). All acronyms defined on first use. No JSON block.

assertions.description: Key acronyms (FCF, WACC, SBC) are defined on first use — case-insensitive match for the full phrase
assertions.pattern: (?i)free cash flow.*weighted.average cost of capital.*stock.based compensation|free cash flow[^\n]{0,200}\(FCF\)|\(WACC\)|weighted.average cost of capital

assertions.description: All five content elements present: bottom line, model build, drivers, risks, trust
Concrete dollar figures appear (current price and at least one IV scenario)
No JSON code block in the output
Risk content is substantive and NVDA-specific
WACC sensitivity is discussed with a quantified dollar/percent impact and connected to interest rates
assertions.pattern: (?i)(WACC|discount rate)[^.]{0,400}(interest rate|rate[s]? ris|1 percentage|one percentage|1 pp|1pp|by \$|to \$|drop[s]? .{0,30}\$|rise[s]? .{0,30}\$)

assertions.description: Concrete implied year-5 FCF figure with a future-year reference
Trust/confidence section includes qualitative business context (market position, lifecycle, kind of bet) rather than only confidence-tier mechanics

evals.expected_output: A friendly narrative for META. Should explain META's FCF (large, advertising-driven), the growth assumption and WACC sensitivity with interest-rate framing, what drives the spread between bear/base/bull, plus the implied year-5 FCF to justify the base estimate. Risks specific to META (ad spend, regulation, Reality Labs capex). Confidence with qualitative context (ad market dynamics, Reality Labs bet). All acronyms defined. No JSON.

assertions.description: Key acronyms (FCF, WACC, SBC) are defined on first use — case-insensitive
assertions.pattern: (?i)free cash flow.*weighted.average cost of capital.*stock.based compensation|free cash flow[^\n]{0,200}\(FCF\)|\(WACC\)|weighted.average cost of capital

assertions.description: All five content elements present: bottom line, model build, drivers, risks, trust
No JSON code block in the output
WACC sensitivity quantified and connected to interest rates
assertions.pattern: (?i)(WACC|discount rate)[^.]{0,400}(interest rate|rate[s]? ris|1 percentage|one percentage|1 pp|1pp|by \$|to \$|drop[s]? .{0,30}\$|rise[s]? .{0,30}\$)

assertions.description: Concrete implied year-5 FCF figure with future-year reference
Trust section includes qualitative business context (ad market, Reality Labs bet, regulatory landscape)

evals.expected_output: A clear error message telling the user no report was found for FAKE, and instructing them to run /stock-signal FAKE and /stock-model FAKE first. Should not crash or produce partial output.

assertions.description: Output contains a helpful error message about no report being found
User is told to run /stock-signal and/or /stock-model first
No valuation sections appear when no report exists
